content:
You are an expert C/C++ software engineer working on the nuttx codebase.
Apache NuttX is a POSIX-compliant real-time operating system for microcontrollers. It has arch-specific startup code, board support packages, character/block device drivers, filesystems, networking (TCP/IP, CAN, I2C, SPI), and scheduling. Pure C codebase.

TOOL USAGE — you MUST use these exact formats:

To run a shell command:
<bash>your_command_here</bash>

To view a file:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>

To view specific lines:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor> [start_line, end_line]

To edit a file (replace text):
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>exact text to replace</old_str>
<new_str>replacement text</new_str>

When finished, say: SUBMIT

IMPORTANT: Each response must contain EXACTLY ONE tool call. Do NOT put multiple tool calls in one response.

Working directory is /repo.
You will be given a PR description. Implement the described changes.

Please implement the following pull request:

## Title: Handle work_queue return values in STM32 SDMMC interrupt handlers

### Summary
This PR addresses potential error conditions in the STM32H7 SDMMC driver where `work_queue` return values were not being checked. Although `sdio_mediachange` itself returns `void`, it calls `stm32_callback`, which may queue work items via `work_queue`. These queued operations can fail silently if `work_queue` returns an error, potentially leading to missed callbacks or unexpected behavior during card detection or data transfer interrupts.

The changes explicitly check the return value of `work_queue` calls in three locations:
1. `stm32_sdmmc_fifo_monitor` - when queuing FIFO monitoring work
2. `stm32_sdmmc_interrupt` - when queuing FIFO monitoring after RX FIFO handling
3. `stm32_callback` - when queuing user callbacks

In each case, an error is logged using `mcerr` if `work_queue` fails, improving debuggability and preventing silent failures.

### Why
While the likelihood of `work_queue` failing is low under normal conditions, checking its return value is a defensive programming practice that helps catch resource exhaustion or system overload scenarios. This makes the driver more robust and provides better diagnostic information when issues occur.

### Affected Files
- `arch/arm/src/stm32h7/stm32_sdmmc.c` - Added error checking for `work_queue` calls in interrupt handlers and callback functions

    60	
    61	/****************************************************************************
    62	 * Pre-processor Definitions
    63	 ****************************************************************************/
    64	
    65	/* Configuration ************************************************************/
    66	
    67	/* HW Issues when not using IDMA
    68	 *
    69	 *    The FIFO lacks a FIFO data available interrupt. It only has a
    70	 * FIFO Half Full and Full interrupt. Given that the FIFO size is 64
    71	 * bytes (16 uint 32), this creates an issue for commands that read
    72	 * less than 32 bytes hereafter referred to as a Lame FIFO.
    73	 *
    74	 * The DATAEND interrupt is only issued on the FIFO being emptied and it
    75	 * appears the DTIMER does not start running again until the FIFO is read
    76	 * so that DTIMEOUT interrupt can not be used to end the read operation.
    77	 *
    78	 * The only workaround is to use the IDMA to service the FIFO and to monitor
    79	 * for a remainder in the FIFO that is less than FIFO_SIZE_IN_BYTES / 2.
    80	 *
    81	 * Therefore even when IDMA is not used, the IDMA still has to be used to
    82	 * service the FIFO to receive less than FIFO_SIZE_IN_BYTES / 2 to a local
    83	 * buffer. When the FIFO Half Full and Full interrupt do fire the FIFO will
    84	 * be monitored off the an HP work thread for a residual of less than
    85	 * FIFO_SIZE_IN_BYTES / 2.
    86	 *
    87	 * HW Issues when using IDMA
    88	 *
    89	 *    The DMA buffer must be located in a zone accessible via IDMA.
    90	 * For SDMMC1, IDMA cannot access SRAM123 or SRAM4. Refer to ST AN520
... [truncated] ...
MMC will work in
   106	 *     interrupt mode and still use the IDMA to a local buffer for data
   107	 *     lengths less the 32 bytes due to the FIFO limitations.
   108	 *   CONFIG_SDMMC1/2_WIDTH_D1_ONLY - This may be selected to force the driver
   109	 *     operate with only a single data line (the default is to use all
   110	 *     4 SD data lines).
   111	 *   CONFIG_STM32H7_SDMMC_XFRDEBUG - Enables some very low-level debug
   112	 *     output This also requires CONFIG_DEBUG_FS and CONFIG_DEBUG_INFO
   113	 *   CONFIG_SDMMC1/2_SDIO_MODE
   114	 *     Build ins additional support needed only for SDIO cards (vs. SD memory
   115	 *     cards)
   116	 *   CONFIG_SDMMC1/2_SDIO_PULLUP
   117	 *      If you are using an external SDCard module that does not have the
   118	 *      pull-up resistors for the SDIO interface (like the Gadgeteer SD Card
   119	 *      Module) then enable this option to activate the internal pull-up
   120	 *      resistors.
   121	 */
   122	
   123	/* If there are 2 SDMMC enabled, then Slot 0 is SDMMC1, and Slot 1 is SDMMC2
   124	 * If there is only 1 SDMMC, then Slot 0 is assigned to the defined SDMMC
   125	 * hence, if only SDMMC2 is defined it will be slot 0.
   126	 */
   127	
   128	#if !defined(CONFIG_STM32H7_SDMMC1)
   129	#  define SDMMC2_SLOT  0
   130	#else
   131	#  define SDMMC2_SLOT  1
   132	#endif
   133	
   134	#if !defined(CONFIG_STM32H7_SDMMC_IDMA)
   135	#  warning "Large Non-DMA transfer may result in RX overrun failures"
   136	#elif defined(CONFIG_STM32H7_SDMMC1)
   137	#  define SRAM123_START STM32_SRAM123_BASE
   138	#  define SRAM123_END   (SRAM123_START + STM32H7_SRAM123_SIZE)
   139	#  define SRAM4_START   STM32_SRAM4_BASE
   140	#  define SRAM4_END     (SRAM4_START + STM32H7_SRAM4_SIZE)
   141	#endif
   142	
   143	#if !defined(CONFIG_SCHED_WORKQUEUE) || !defined(CONFIG_SCHED_HPWORK)
   144	#  error "Callback support requires CONFIG_SCHED_WORKQUEUE and CONFIG_SCHED_HPWORK"
   145	#endif
   146	
   147	#undef HAVE_SDMMC_SDIO_MODE
   148	#if defined(CONFIG_SDMMC1_SDIO_MODE) || defined(CONFIG_SDMMC2_SDIO_MODE)
   149	#  define HAVE_SDMMC_SDIO_MODE
   150	#endif
   151	
   152	#if !defined(CONFIG_DEBUG_FS) || !defined(CONFIG_DEBUG_FEATURES)
   153	#  undef CONFIG_STM32H7_SDMMC_XFRDEBUG
   154	#endif
   155	
   156	#ifdef CONFIG_SDMMC1_SDIO_PULLUP
   157	#  define SDMMC1_SDIO_PULL(g)  (((g) & ~GPIO_PUPD_MASK) | GPIO_PULLUP)
   158	#else
   159	#  define SDMMC1_SDIO_PULL(g)  (((g) & ~GPIO_PUPD_MASK) | GPIO_FLOAT)
   160	#endif
   161	
   162	#ifdef CONFIG_SDMMC2_SDIO_PULLUP

   166	#endif

   168	/* Define the Hardware FIFO size */

   180	#endif

   184	#else
   185	#  define STM32_SDMMC_CLK  STM32_PLL2R_FREQUENCY
   186	#endif
   187

   189	#define STM32_CLKCR_FALLINGEDGE   STM32_SDMMC_CLKCR_NEGEDGE
   190

   192	 * that does not have the errata, to override the edge the SDIO
   193	 * command and data is changed on.
   194	 */
   195	
   196	#if !defined(STM32_SDMMC_CLKCR_EDGE)
   197	#  define STM32_SDMMC_CLKCR_EDGE STM32_CLKCR_RISINGEDGE
   198	#endif
